root_cause.explanation: The upload pipeline at src/main/media/upload-pipeline.ts:275 blindly trusts the server-provided URL from the Blossom response body without considering that the server's view of its own hostname may differ from how clients reach it (Docker internal names, reverse proxies, NAT, etc.)
sequence: Pipeline stores unreachable URL in message_media.remote_url
Renderer fails to load image with ERR_NAME_NOT_RESOLVED
evidence: Line 275: const remoteUrl = body.url || body.nurl; - uses server response directly
Line 227: const server = await this.deps.selectHealthyServer(...) - has client-configured URL
Line 240: const uploadUrl = `${server.url}/upload` - proves client knows correct base URL

changes.description: Extract blob hash from response (body.sha256 or parse from body.url), then construct URL as ${server.url}/blob/${hash} using client-configured server.url
Update test expectations and add regression test

invariants.existing_behavior: All upload features unchanged except URL construction
invariants.backward_compatibility: Old uploads with reachable URLs still work

fix_approach: Extract blob hash from Blossom response (prefer body.sha256, fallback to parsing from body.url). Construct retrieval URL as ${server.url}/blob/${hash} using the client-configured server.url that's already available in function scope (line 227). This ensures the URL is reachable from the client's network perspective regardless of server's internal hostname.
verification_criteria: All 124 baseline test suites pass (no regressions)
Modified files limited to upload-pipeline.ts and upload-pipeline.test.ts
